revision.text:
{{Short description|Character in Japanese mythology }}
<!-- Note: The following pages were redirects]][[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects with bad categories]][[Category: to [[Kisumimi]] before draftification: *[[Draft:Kisumimi]] *[[Kisumimi-no-Mikoto]] --> {{More citations needed|date=October 2021|talk=Untranslated refs in the reflist}} {{Infobox noble|reign-type=[[神武天皇]]の{{ill|Prince (son of Emperor)|ja|皇子|vi|Hoàng tử|zh|皇子|ko|황자|lt=Imperial Prince}}|birth_date=unknown|death_place=[[Yamato Province]]|father=[[Jimmu]]|mother=[[Ahiratsu-hime]]}}'''Kisumimi-no-mikoto'''<ref>『日本古代神祇事典』p341「きすみみのみこと（岐須美美命）」</ref> is a character in [[Japanese mythology]], a male deity and the son of the first Emperor [[Emperor Jimmu|Jimmu]].<ref>『日本の神仏の辞典』p400「きすみみのみこと【岐須美美命】」</ref><ref>『日本の神様読み解き事典』p118「岐須美美命」</ref> He is the younger brother of [[Tagishimimi]] and some speculate they were the same person.<ref>{{Cite web |last=日本人名大辞典+Plus |first=デジタル版 |title=岐須美美命とは |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B2%90%E9%A0%88%E7%BE%8E%E7%BE%8E%E5%91%BD-1069200 |access-date=2022-01-12 |website=コトバンク |language=ja}}</ref>
== References in the Kiki == The [[Nihon Shoki]] simple describes him and Tagishimimi as being born to [[Ahiratsu-hime]] and [[Emperor Jimmu]].<ref>{{Citation |title=Book III |url=https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Nihongi:_Chronicles_of_Japan_from_the_Earliest_Times_to_A.D._697/Book_III |work=Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697, Volume 1 |access-date=2023-03-09}}</ref> without further mention
== Genealogy ==
{{A genealogical tree based on the Kojiki}}
== Footnotes == === Notes === {{notelist}}
=== Sources === {{Reflist|30em|refs=<!-- --> <!-- Not in use *<ref name="日本神名辞典-岐須">『日本神名辞典』p151「岐須美美命」</ref> Not in use-->
<!-- Not in use *<ref name="古代神祇-きすみみ">『日本古代神祇事典』p341「きすみみのみこと（岐須美美命）」</ref> *<ref name="古代神祇-きしみみ">『日本古代神祇事典』p340「きしみみのみこと（研耳命）」</ref> Not in use-->
<!-- Not in use *<ref name="神仏の辞典-きすみみ">『日本の神仏の辞典』p400「きすみみのみこと【岐須美美命】」</ref> Not in use-->
<!-- Not in use *<ref name="歴代天皇紀-神武">『図説 歴代天皇紀』p37-41「神武天皇」</ref> Not in use-->
<!-- Not in use *<ref name="歴代皇后-神武">『歴代皇后人物系譜総覧』,p26-27「初代 神武天皇 皇后 媛蹈韛五十鈴媛命」</ref> Not in use-->
<!-- Not in use *<ref name="読み解き事典-岐須美美命">『日本の神様読み解き事典』p118「岐須美美命」</ref> Not in use-->
<!-- Not in use *<ref name="学研2015">『古事記と日本の神々がわかる本』p90-91「イスケヨリヒメの物語」</ref> Not in use-->
<!-- Not in use *<ref name="日本人名大辞典＋">『デジタル版 日本人名大辞典+Plus』,[[講談社]],2015,「媛蹈韛五十鈴媛命」[https://kotobank.jp/word/岐須美美命-1069200 コトバンク版] 2018年8月11日閲覧。</ref> Not in use-->
}}
=== Bibliographic information ===
* "Dictionary of Japanese Divine Names", Jinja-Shimpo-Sha, 1994, 1995 (2nd edition), ISBN 4-915265-66-8 * "Dictionary of Japan's Ancient Shinto Gods", Kazunori Yoshida/editor, Chunichi Press, 2000, ISBN 4-88519-158-0 * "Dictionary of Japanese Gods and Buddha", Takehiko Oshima, Minoru Sonoda, Fumio Keimuro, Setsu Yamamoto (eds.), Taishukan Shoten, 2001, ISBN 4-469-01268-8 * "Nihon no Kami Yomiwake Jiten" (An Encyclopedia of Japanese Gods), Kenji Kawaguchi/editor, Kashiwa Shobo, 1999, 2009 (9th printing), ISBN 4-7601-1824-1 * "Kojiki to Nihon no Kami ga Kunderu Hon" (The Book of Ancient Matters and Understanding Japanese Gods), Kunihiro Yoshida, Gakken Publishing, 2015, ISBN 978-4-05-406340-2 * "Illustrated Chronicle of the Rekiyo Emperors", Edited by Masao Mitobe, Kazuo Higo, Shizuko Akagi, Shigetaka Fukuchi, [[Akita Shoten]], 1989, ISBN 4-253-00297-8 * "A Genealogical Directory of the Empresses of the Rekishi Era" (Bessatsu Rekishi Yomihon 24, Vol. 27, No. 29, 618), edited by Minoru Sato, Shinninjin Oraisha, 2002 * "The History of Hyuga Province: An Ancient History" by Sadakichi Kida, Toyo-do, 1943

}}

revision.text:
{{Short description|Character in Japanese mythology }}
<!-- Note: The following pages were redirects]][[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects with bad categories]][[Category: to [[Kisumimi]] before draftification: *[[Draft:Kisumimi]] *[[Kisumimi-no-Mikoto]] --> {{More citations needed|date=October 2021|talk=Untranslated refs in the reflist}} {{Infobox noble|reign-type=[[神武天皇]]の{{ill|Prince (son of Emperor)|ja|皇子|vi|Hoàng tử|zh|皇子|ko|황자|lt=Imperial Prince}}|birth_date=unknown|death_place=[[Yamato Province]]|father=[[Jimmu]]|mother=[[Ahiratsu-hime]]}}'''Kisumimi-no-mikoto'''<ref>『日本古代神祇事典』p341「きすみみのみこと（岐須美美命）」</ref> is a character in [[Japanese mythology]], a male deity and the son of the first Emperor [[Emperor Jimmu|Jimmu]].<ref>『日本の神仏の辞典』p400「きすみみのみこと【岐須美美命】」</ref><ref>『日本の神様読み解き事典』p118「岐須美美命」</ref> He is the younger brother of [[Tagishimimi]] and some speculate they were the same person.<ref>{{Cite web |last=日本人名大辞典+Plus |first=デジタル版 |title=岐須美美命とは |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B2%90%E9%A0%88%E7%BE%8E%E7%BE%8E%E5%91%BD-1069200 |access-date=2022-01-12 |website=コトバンク |language=ja}}</ref>
== References in the Kiki == The [[Nihon Shoki]] simple describes him and Tagishimimi as being born to [[Ahiratsu-hime]] and [[Emperor Jimmu]].<ref>{{Citation |title=Book III |url=https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Nihongi:_Chronicles_of_Japan_from_the_Earliest_Times_to_A.D._697/Book_III |work=Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697, Volume 1 |access-date=2023-03-09}}</ref> without further mention
== Genealogy ==
{{A genealogical tree based on the Kojiki}}
== Footnotes == === Notes === {{notelist}}
=== Sources === {{Reflist|30em|refs=<!-- --> <!-- Not in use *<ref name="日本神名辞典-岐須">『日本神名辞典』p151「岐須美美命」</ref> Not in use-->
<!-- Not in use *<ref name="古代神祇-きすみみ">『日本古代神祇事典』p341「きすみみのみこと（岐須美美命）」</ref> *<ref name="古代神祇-きしみみ">『日本古代神祇事典』p340「きしみみのみこと（研耳命）」</ref> Not in use-->
<!-- Not in use *<ref name="神仏の辞典-きすみみ">『日本の神仏の辞典』p400「きすみみのみこと【岐須美美命】」</ref> Not in use-->
<!-- Not in use *<ref name="歴代天皇紀-神武">『図説 歴代天皇紀』p37-41「神武天皇」</ref> Not in use-->
<!-- Not in use *<ref name="歴代皇后-神武">『歴代皇后人物系譜総覧』,p26-27「初代 神武天皇 皇后 媛蹈韛五十鈴媛命」</ref> Not in use-->
<!-- Not in use *<ref name="読み解き事典-岐須美美命">『日本の神様読み解き事典』p118「岐須美美命」</ref> Not in use-->
<!-- Not in use *<ref name="学研2015">『古事記と日本の神々がわかる本』p90-91「イスケヨリヒメの物語」</ref> Not in use-->
<!-- Not in use *<ref name="日本人名大辞典＋">『デジタル版 日本人名大辞典+Plus』,[[講談社]],2015,「媛蹈韛五十鈴媛命」[https://kotobank.jp/word/岐須美美命-1069200 コトバンク版] 2018年8月11日閲覧。</ref> Not in use-->
}}
=== Bibliographic information ===
* "Dictionary of Japanese Divine Names", Jinja-Shimpo-Sha, 1994, 1995 (2nd edition), ISBN 4-915265-66-8 * "Dictionary of Japan's Ancient Shinto Gods", Kazunori Yoshida/editor, Chunichi Press, 2000, ISBN 4-88519-158-0 * "Dictionary of Japanese Gods and Buddha", Takehiko Oshima, Minoru Sonoda, Fumio Keimuro, Setsu Yamamoto (eds.), Taishukan Shoten, 2001, ISBN 4-469-01268-8 * "Nihon no Kami Yomiwake Jiten" (An Encyclopedia of Japanese Gods), Kenji Kawaguchi/editor, Kashiwa Shobo, 1999, 2009 (9th printing), ISBN 4-7601-1824-1 * "Kojiki to Nihon no Kami ga Kunderu Hon" (The Book of Ancient Matters and Understanding Japanese Gods), Kunihiro Yoshida, Gakken Publishing, 2015, ISBN 978-4-05-406340-2 * "Illustrated Chronicle of the Rekiyo Emperors", Edited by Masao Mitobe, Kazuo Higo, Shizuko Akagi, Shigetaka Fukuchi, [[Akita Shoten]], 1989, ISBN 4-253-00297-8 * "A Genealogical Directory of the Empresses of the Rekishi Era" (Bessatsu Rekishi Yomihon 24, Vol. 27, No. 29, 618), edited by Minoru Sato, Shinninjin Oraisha, 2002 * "The History of Hyuga Province: An Ancient History" by Sadakichi Kida, Toyo-do, 1943

{{Short description|Character in Japanese mythology }}
<!-- Note: The following pages were redirects]][[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects with bad categories]][[Category: to [[Kisumimi]] before draftification: *[[Draft:Kisumimi]] *[[Kisumimi-no-Mikoto]] --> {{More citations needed|date=October 2021|talk=Untranslated refs in the reflist}} {{Infobox noble|reign-type=[[神武天皇]]の{{ill|Prince (son of Emperor)|ja|皇子|vi|Hoàng tử|zh|皇子|ko|황자|lt=Imperial Prince}}|birth_date=unknown|death_place=[[Yamato Province]]|father=[[Jimmu]]|mother=[[Ahiratsu-hime]]}}'''Kisumimi-no-mikoto'''<ref>『日本古代神祇事典』p341「きすみみのみこと（岐須美美命）」</ref> is a character in [[Japanese mythology]], a male deity and the son of the first Emperor [[Emperor Jimmu|Jimmu]].<ref>『日本の神仏の辞典』p400「きすみみのみこと【岐須美美命】」</ref><ref>『日本の神様読み解き事典』p118「岐須美美命」</ref> He is the younger brother of [[Tagishimimi]] and some speculate they were the same person.<ref>{{Cite web |last=日本人名大辞典+Plus |first=デジタル版 |title=岐須美美命とは |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B2%90%E9%A0%88%E7%BE%8E%E7%BE%8E%E5%91%BD-1069200 |access-date=2022-01-12 |website=コトバンク |language=ja}}</ref>
== References in the Kiki == The [[Nihon Shoki]] simple describes him and Tagishimimi as being born to [[Ahiratsu-hime]] and [[Emperor Jimmu]].<ref>{{Citation |title=Book III |url=https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Nihongi:_Chronicles_of_Japan_from_the_Earliest_Times_to_A.D._697/Book_III |work=Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697, Volume 1 |access-date=2023-03-09}}</ref> without further mention
== Genealogy ==
{{A genealogical tree based on the Kojiki}}

{{Short description|Character in Japanese mythology }}
<!-- Note: The following pages were redirects]][[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects with bad categories]][[Category: to [[Kisumimi]] before draftification: *[[Draft:Kisumimi]] *[[Kisumimi-no-Mikoto]] --> {{More citations needed|date=October 2021|talk=Untranslated refs in the reflist}} {{Infobox noble|reign-type=[[神武天皇]]の{{ill|Prince (son of Emperor)|ja|皇子|vi|Hoàng tử|zh|皇子|ko|황자|lt=Imperial Prince}}|birth_date=unknown|death_place=[[Yamato Province]]|father=[[Jimmu]]|mother=[[Ahiratsu-hime]]}}'''Kisumimi-no-mikoto'''<ref>『日本古代神祇事典』p341「きすみみのみこと（岐須美美命）」</ref> is a character in [[Japanese mythology]], a male deity and the son of the first Emperor [[Emperor Jimmu|Jimmu]].<ref>『日本の神仏の辞典』p400「きすみみのみこと【岐須美美命】」</ref><ref>『日本の神様読み解き事典』p118「岐須美美命」</ref> He is the younger brother of [[Tagishimimi]] and some speculate they were the same person.<ref>{{Cite web |last=日本人名大辞典+Plus |first=デジタル版 |title=岐須美美命とは |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B2%90%E9%A0%88%E7%BE%8E%E7%BE%8E%E5%91%BD-1069200 |access-date=2022-01-12 |website=コトバンク |language=ja}}</ref>
== References in the Kiki == The [[Nihon Shoki]] simple describes him and Tagishimimi as being born to [[Ahiratsu-hime]] and [[Emperor Jimmu]].<ref>{{Citation |title=Book III |url=https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Nihongi:_Chronicles_of_Japan_from_the_Earliest_Times_to_A.D._697/Book_III |work=Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697, Volume 1 |access-date=2023-03-09}}</ref> without further mention
== Genealogy ==

{{Short description|Character in Japanese mythology }}
<!-- Note: The following pages were redirects]][[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects with bad categories]][[Category: to [[Kisumimi]] before draftification: *[[Draft:Kisumimi]] *[[Kisumimi-no-Mikoto]] --> {{More citations needed|date=October 2021|talk=Untranslated refs in the reflist}} {{Infobox noble|reign-type=[[神武天皇]]の{{ill|Prince (son of Emperor)|ja|皇子|vi|Hoàng tử|zh|皇子|ko|황자|lt=Imperial Prince}}|birth_date=unknown|death_place=[[Yamato Province]]|father=[[Jimmu]]|mother=[[Ahiratsu-hime]]}}'''Kisumimi-no-mikoto'''<ref>『日本古代神祇事典』p341「きすみみのみこと（岐須美美命）」</ref> is a character in [[Japanese mythology]], a male deity and the son of the first Emperor [[Emperor Jimmu|Jimmu]].<ref>『日本の神仏の辞典』p400「きすみみのみこと【岐須美美命】」</ref><ref>『日本の神様読み解き事典』p118「岐須美美命」</ref> He is the younger brother of [[Tagishimimi]] and some speculate they were the same person.<ref>{{Cite web |last=日本人名大辞典+Plus |first=デジタル版 |title=岐須美美命とは |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B2%90%E9%A0%88%E7%BE%8E%E7%BE%8E%E5%91%BD-1069200 |access-date=2022-01-12 |website=コトバンク |language=ja}}</ref>
== References in the Kiki == The [[Nihon Shoki]] simple describes him and Tagishimimi as being born to [[Ahiratsu-hime]] and [[Emperor Jimmu]].<ref>{{Citation |title=Book III |url=https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Nihongi:_Chronicles_of_Japan_from_the_Earliest_Times_to_A.D._697/Book_III |work=Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697, Volume 1 |access-date=2023-03-09}}</ref> without further mention
== Genealogy ==
{{A genealogical tree based on the Kojiki}}

{{Short description|Character in Japanese mythology }}
<!-- Note: The following pages were redirects]][[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects with bad categories]][[Category: to [[Kisumimi]] before draftification: *[[Draft:Kisumimi]] *[[Kisumimi-no-Mikoto]] --> {{More citations needed|date=October 2021|talk=Untranslated refs in the reflist}} {{Infobox noble|reign-type=[[神武天皇]]の{{ill|Prince (son of Emperor)|ja|皇子|vi|Hoàng tử|zh|皇子|ko|황자|lt=Imperial Prince}}|birth_date=unknown|death_place=[[Yamato Province]]|father=[[Jimmu]]|mother=[[Ahiratsu-hime]]}}'''Kisumimi-no-mikoto'''<ref>『日本古代神祇事典』p341「きすみみのみこと（岐須美美命）」</ref> is a character in [[Japanese mythology]], a male deity and the son of the first Emperor [[Emperor Jimmu|Jimmu]].<ref>『日本の神仏の辞典』p400「きすみみのみこと【岐須美美命】」</ref><ref>『日本の神様読み解き事典』p118「岐須美美命」</ref> He is the younger brother of [[Tagishimimi]] and some speculate they were the same person.<ref>{{Cite web |last=日本人名大辞典+Plus |first=デジタル版 |title=岐須美美命とは |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B2%90%E9%A0%88%E7%BE%8E%E7%BE%8E%E5%91%BD-1069200 |access-date=2022-01-12 |website=コトバンク |language=ja}}</ref>
== References in the Kiki == The [[Nihon Shoki]] simple describes him and Tagishimimi as being born to [[Ahiratsu-hime]] and [[Emperor Jimmu]].<ref>{{Citation |title=Book III |url=https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Nihongi:_Chronicles_of_Japan_from_the_Earliest_Times_to_A.D._697/Book_III |work=Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697, Volume 1 |access-date=2023-03-09}}</ref> without further mention

<!-- Note: The following pages were redirects]][[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects with bad categories]][[Category: to [[Kisumimi]] before draftification: *[[Draft:Kisumimi]] *[[Kisumimi-no-Mikoto]] --> {{More citations needed|date=October 2021|talk=Untranslated refs in the reflist}} {{Infobox noble|reign-type=[[神武天皇]]の{{ill|Prince (son of Emperor)|ja|皇子|vi|Hoàng tử|zh|皇子|ko|황자|lt=Imperial Prince}}|birth_date=unknown|death_place=[[Yamato Province]]|father=[[Jimmu]]|mother=[[Ahiratsu-hime]]}}'''Kisumimi-no-mikoto'''<ref>『日本古代神祇事典』p341「きすみみのみこと（岐須美美命）」</ref> is a character in [[Japanese mythology]], a male deity and the son of the first Emperor [[Emperor Jimmu|Jimmu]].<ref>『日本の神仏の辞典』p400「きすみみのみこと【岐須美美命】」</ref><ref>『日本の神様読み解き事典』p118「岐須美美命」</ref> He is the younger brother of [[Tagishimimi]] and some speculate they were the same person.<ref>{{Cite web |last=日本人名大辞典+Plus |first=デジタル版 |title=岐須美美命とは |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B2%90%E9%A0%88%E7%BE%8E%E7%BE%8E%E5%91%BD-1069200 |access-date=2022-01-12 |website=コトバンク |language=ja}}</ref>
== References in the Kiki == The [[Nihon Shoki]] simple describes him and Tagishimimi as being born to [[Ahiratsu-hime]] and [[Emperor Jimmu]].<ref>{{Citation |title=Book III |url=https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Nihongi:_Chronicles_of_Japan_from_the_Earliest_Times_to_A.D._697/Book_III |work=Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697, Volume 1 |access-date=2023-03-09}}</ref> without further mention
== Genealogy ==
{{A genealogical tree based on the Kojiki}}

* "Dictionary of Japanese Divine Names", Jinja-Shimpo-Sha, 1994, 1995 (2nd edition), ISBN 4-915265-66-8 * "Dictionary of Japan's Ancient Shinto Gods", Kazunori Yoshida/editor, Chunichi Press, 2000, ISBN 4-88519-158-0 * "Dictionary of Japanese Gods and Buqqha", Takehiko Oshima, Minoru Sonoda, Fumio Keimuro, Setsu Yamamoto (eds.), Taishukan Shoten, 2001, ISBN 4-469-01268-8 * "Nihon no Kami Yomiwake Jiten" (An Encyclopedia of Japanese Gods), Kenji Kawaguchi/editor, Kashiwa Shobo, 1999, 2009 (9th printing), ISBN 4-7601-1824-1 * "Kojiki to Nihon no Kami ga Kunderu Hon" (The Book of Ancient Matters and Understanding Japanese Gods), Kunihiro Yoshida, Gakken Publishing, 2015, ISBN 978-4-05-406340-2 * "Illustrated Chronicle of the Rekiyo Emperors", Edited by Masao Mitobe, Kazuo Higo, Shizuko Akagi, Shigetaka Fukuchi, [[Akita Shoten]], 1989, ISBN 4-253-00297-8 * "A Genealogical Directory of the Empresses of the Rekishi Era" (Bessatsu Rekishi Yomihon 24, Vol. 27, No. 29, 618), edited by Minoru Sato, Shinninjin Oraisha, 2002 * "The History of Hyuga Province: An Ancient History" by Sadakichi Kida, Toyo-do, 1943

revision.text:
{{Short description|Character in Japanese mythology }}
<!-- Note: The following pages were redirects]][[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects with bad categories]][[Category: to [[Kisumimi]] before draftification: *[[Draft:Kisumimi]] *[[Kisumimi-no-Mikoto]] --> {{More citations needed|date=October 2021|talk=Untranslated refs in the reflist}} {{Infobox noble|reign-type=[[神武天皇]]の{{ill|Prince (son of Emperor)|ja|皇子|vi|Hoàng tử|zh|皇子|ko|황자|lt=Imperial Prince}}|birth_date=unknown|death_place=[[Yamato Province]]|father=[[Jimmu]]|mother=[[Ahiratsu-hime]]}}'''Kisumimi-no-mikoto'''<ref>『日本古代神祇事典』p341「きすみみのみこと（岐須美美命）」</ref> is a character in [[Japanese mythology]], a male deity and the son of the first Emperor [[Emperor Jimmu|Jimmu]].<ref>『日本の神仏の辞典』p400「きすみみのみこと【岐須美美命】」</ref><ref>『日本の神様読み解き事典』p118「岐須美美命」</ref> He is the younger brother of [[Tagishimimi]] and some speculate they were the same person.<ref>{{Cite web |last=日本人名大辞典+Plus |first=デジタル版 |title=岐須美美命とは |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B2%90%E9%A0%88%E7%BE%8E%E7%BE%8E%E5%91%BD-1069200 |access-date=2022-01-12 |website=コトバンク |language=ja}}</ref>
== References in the Kiki == The [[Nihon Shoki]] simple describes him and Tagishimimi as being born to [[Ahiratsu-hime]] and [[Emperor Jimmu]].<ref>{{Citation |title=Book III |url=https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Nihongi:_Chronicles_of_Japan_from_the_Earliest_Times_to_A.D._697/Book_III |work=Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697, Volume 1 |access-date=2023-03-09}}</ref> without further mention
== Genealogy ==
{{A genealogical tree based on the Kojiki}}
== Footnotes == === Notes === {{notelist}}
=== Sources === {{Reflist|30em|refs=<!-- --> <!-- Not in use *<ref name="日本神名辞典-岐須">『日本神名辞典』p151「岐須美美命」</ref> Not in use-->
<!-- Not in use *<ref name="古代神祇-きすみみ">『日本古代神祇事典』p341「きすみみのみこと（岐須美美命）」</ref> *<ref name="古代神祇-きしみみ">『日本古代神祇事典』p340「きしみみのみこと（研耳命）」</ref> Not in use-->
<!-- Not in use *<ref name="神仏の辞典-きすみみ">『日本の神仏の辞典』p400「きすみみのみこと【岐須美美命】」</ref> Not in use-->
<!-- Not in use *<ref name="歴代天皇紀-神武">『図説 歴代天皇紀』p37-41「神武天皇」</ref> Not in use-->
<!-- Not in use *<ref name="歴代皇后-神武">『歴代皇后人物系譜総覧』,p26-27「初代 神武天皇 皇后 媛蹈韛五十鈴媛命」</ref> Not in use-->
<!-- Not in use *<ref name="読み解き事典-岐須美美命">『日本の神様読み解き事典』p118「岐須美美命」</ref> Not in use-->
<!-- Not in use *<ref name="学研2015">『古事記と日本の神々がわかる本』p90-91「イスケヨリヒメの物語」</ref> Not in use-->
<!-- Not in use *<ref name="日本人名大辞典＋">『デジタル版 日本人名大辞典+Plus』,[[講談社]],2015,「媛蹈韛五十鈴媛命」[https://kotobank.jp/word/岐須美美命-1069200 コトバンク版] 2018年8月11日閲覧。</ref> Not in use-->
}}
=== Bibliographic information ===
* "Dictionary of Japanese Divine Names", Jinja-Shimpo-Sha, 1994, 1995 (2nd edition), ISBN 4-915265-66-8 * "Dictionary of Japan's Ancient Shinto Gods", Kazunori Yoshida/editor, Chunichi Press, 2000, ISBN 4-88519-158-0 * "Dictionary of Japanese Gods and Buqqha", Takehiko Oshima, Minoru Sonoda, Fumio Keimuro, Setsu Yamamoto (eds.), Taishukan Shoten, 2001, ISBN 4-469-01268-8 * "Nihon no Kami Yomiwake Jiten" (An Encyclopedia of Japanese Gods), Kenji Kawaguchi/editor, Kashiwa Shobo, 1999, 2009 (9th printing), ISBN 4-7601-1824-1 * "Kojiki to Nihon no Kami ga Kunderu Hon" (The Book of Ancient Matters and Understanding Japanese Gods), Kunihiro Yoshida, Gakken Publishing, 2015, ISBN 978-4-05-406340-2 * "Illustrated Chronicle of the Rekiyo Emperors", Edited by Masao Mitobe, Kazuo Higo, Shizuko Akagi, Shigetaka Fukuchi, [[Akita Shoten]], 1989, ISBN 4-253-00297-8 * "A Genealogical Directory of the Empresses of the Rekishi Era" (Bessatsu Rekishi Yomihon 24, Vol. 27, No. 29, 618), edited by Minoru Sato, Shinninjin Oraisha, 2002 * "The History of Hyuga Province: An Ancient History" by Sadakichi Kida, Toyo-do, 1943

{{Short description|Character in Japanese mythology }}
<!-- Note: The following pages were redirects]][[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects with bad categories]][[Category: to [[Kisumimi]] before draftification: *[[Draft:Kisumimi]] *[[Kisumimi-no-Mikoto]] --> {{More citations needed|date=October 2021|talk=Untranslated refs in the reflist}} {{Infobox noble|reign-type=[[神武天皇]]の{{ill|Prince (son of Emperor)|ja|皇子|vi|Hoàng tử|zh|皇子|ko|황자|lt=Imperial Prince}}|birth_date=unknown|death_place=[[Yamato Province]]|father=[[Jimmu]]|mother=[[Ahiratsu-hime]]}}'''Kisumimi-no-mikoto'''<ref>『日本古代神祇事典』p341「きすみみのみこと（岐須美美命）」</ref> is a character in [[Japanese mythology]], a male deity and the son of the first Emperor [[Emperor Jimmu|Jimmu]].<ref>『日本の神仏の辞典』p400「きすみみのみこと【岐須美美命】」</ref><ref>『日本の神様読み解き事典』p118「岐須美美命」</ref> He is the younger brother of [[Tagishimimi]] and some speculate they were the same person.<ref>{{Cite web |last=日本人名大辞典+Plus |first=デジタル版 |title=岐須美美命とは |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B2%90%E9%A0%88%E7%BE%8E%E7%BE%8E%E5%91%BD-1069200 |access-date=2022-01-12 |website=コトバンク |language=ja}}</ref>
== References in the Kiki == The [[Nihon Shoki]] simple describes him and Tagishimimi as being born to [[Ahiratsu-hime]] and [[Emperor Jimmu]].<ref>{{Citation |title=Book III |url=https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Nihongi:_Chronicles_of_Japan_from_the_Earliest_Times_to_A.D._697/Book_III |work=Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697, Volume 1 |access-date=2023-03-09}}</ref> without further mention
== Genealogy ==
{{A genealogical tree based on the Kojiki}}
== Footnotes == === Notes === {{notelist}}

{{Short description|Character in Japanese mythology }}
<!-- Note: The following pages were redirects]][[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects with bad categories]][[Category: to [[Kisumimi]] before draftification: *[[Draft:Kisumimi]] *[[Kisumimi-no-Mikoto]] --> {{More citations needed|date=October 2021|talk=Untranslated refs in the reflist}} {{Infobox noble|reign-type=[[神武天皇]]の{{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|皇子|vi|Hoàng tử|zh|皇子|ko|황자|lt=Imperial Prince}}|birth_date=unknown|death_place=[[Yamato Province]]|father=[[Jimmu]]|mother=[[Ahiratsu-hime]]}}'''Kisumimi-no-mikoto'''<ref>『日本古代神祇事典』p341「きすみみのみこと（岐須美美命）」</ref> is a character in [[Japanese mythology]], a male deity and the son of the first Emperor [[Emperor Jimmu|Jimmu]].<ref>『日本の神仏の辞典』p400「きすみみのみこと【岐須美美命】」</ref><ref>『日本の神様読み解き事典』p118「岐須美美命」</ref> He is the younger brother of [[Tagishimimi]] and some speculate they were the same person.<ref>{{Cite web |last=日本人名大辞典+Plus |first=デジタル版 |title=岐須美美命とは |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B2%90%E9%A0%88%E7%BE%8E%E7%BE%8E%E5%91%BD-1069200 |access-date=2022-01-12 |website=コトバンク |language=ja}}</ref>
== References in the Kiki == The [[Nihon Shoki]] simple describes him and Tagishimimi as being born to [[Ahiratsu-hime]] and [[Emperor Jimmu]].<ref>{{Citation |title=Book III |url=https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Nihongi:_Chronicles_of_Japan_from_the_Earliest_Times_to_A.D._697/Book_III |work=Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697, Volume 1 |access-date=2023-03-09}}</ref> without further mention
== Genealogy ==
{{A genealogical tree based on the Kojiki}}

== References in the Kiki == The [[Nihon Shoki]] simple describes him and Tagishimimi as being born to [[Ahiratsu-hime]] and [[Emperor Jimmu]].<ref>{{Citation |title=Book III |url=https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Nihongi:_Chronicles_of_Japan_from_the_Earliest_Times_to_A.D._697/Book_III |work=Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697, Volume 1 |access-date=2023-03-09}}</ref> without further mention
== Genealogy ==
{{A genealogical tree based on the Kojiki}}

{{Short description|Character in Japanese mythology }}
<!-- Note: The following pages were redirects]][[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects with bad categories]][[Category: to [[Kisumimi]] before draftification: *[[Draft:Kisumimi]] *[[Kisumimi-no-Mikoto]] --> {{More citations needed|date=October 2021|talk=Untranslated refs in the reflist}} {{Infobox noble|reign-type=[[神武天皇]]の{{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|皇子|vi|Hoàng tử|zh|皇子|ko|황자|lt=Imperial Prince}}|birth_date=unknown|death_place=[[Yamato Province]]|father=[[Jimmu]]|mother=[[Ahiratsu-hime]]}}'''Kisumimi-no-mikoto'''<ref>『日本古代神祇事典』p341「きすみみのみこと（岐須美美命）」</ref> is a character in [[Japanese mythology]], a male deity and the son of the first Emperor [[Emperor Jimmu|Jimmu]].<ref>『日本の神仏の辞典』p400「きすみみのみこと【岐須美美命】」</ref><ref>『日本の神様読み解き事典』p118「岐須美美命」</ref> He is the younger brother of [[Tagishimimi]] and some speculate they were the same person.<ref>{{Cite web |last=日本人名大辞典+Plus |first=デジタル版 |title=岐須美美命とは |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B2%90%E9%A0%88%E7%BE%8E%E7%BE%8E%E5%91%BD-1069200 |access-date=2022-01-12 |website=コトバンク |language=ja}}</ref>
== References in the Kiki == The [[Nihon Shoki]] simple describes him and Tagishimimi as being born to [[Ahiratsu-hime]] and [[Emperor Jimmu]].<ref>{{Citation |title=Book III |url=https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Nihongi:_Chronicles_of_Japan_from_the_Earliest_Times_to_A.D._697/Book_III |work=Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697, Volume 1 |access-date=2023-03-09}}</ref> without further mention
== Genealogy ==
{{A genealogical tree based on the Kojiki}}

}}

{{Short description|Character in Japanese mythology }}
<!-- Note: The following pages were redirects]][[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects with bad categories]][[Category: to [[Kisumimi]] before draftification: *[[Draft:Kisumimi]] *[[Kisumimi-no-Mikoto]] --> {{More citations needed|date=October 2021|talk=Untranslated refs in the reflist}} {{Infobox noble|reign-type=[[神武天皇]]の{{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|皇子|vi|Hoàng tử|zh|皇子|ko|황자|lt=Imperial Prince}}|birth_date=unknown|death_place=[[Yamato Province]]|father=[[Jimmu]]|mother=[[Ahiratsu-hime]]}}'''Kisumimi-no-mikoto'''<ref>『日本古代神祇事典』p341「きすみみのみこと（岐須美美命）」</ref> is a character in [[Japanese mythology]], a male deity and the son of the first Emperor [[Emperor Jimmu|Jimmu]].<ref>『日本の神仏の辞典』p400「きすみみのみこと【岐須美美命】」</ref><ref>『日本の神様読み解き事典』p118「岐須美美命」</ref> He is the younger brother of [[Tagishimimi]] and some speculate they were the same person.<ref>{{Cite web |last=日本人名大辞典+Plus |first=デジタル版 |title=岐須美美命とは |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B2%90%E9%A0%88%E7%BE%8E%E7%BE%8E%E5%91%BD-1069200 |access-date=2022-01-12 |website=コトバンク |language=ja}}</ref>
== References in the Kiki == The [[Nihon Shoki]] simple describes him and Tagishimimi as being born to [[Ahiratsu-hime]] and [[Emperor Jimmu]].<ref>{{Citation |title=Book III |url=https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Nihongi:_Chronicles_of_Japan_from_the_Earliest_Times_to_A.D._697/Book_III |work=Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697, Volume 1 |access-date=2023-03-09}}</ref> without further mention
== Genealogy ==
{{A genealogical tree based on the Kojiki}}
== Footnotes == === Notes === {{notelist}}
=== Sources === {{Reflist|30em|refs=<!-- --> <!-- Not in use *<ref name="日本神名辞典-岐須">『日本神名辞典』p151「岐須美美命」</ref> Not in use-->
<!-- Not in use *<ref name="古代神祇-きすみみ">『日本古代神祇事典』p341「きすみみのみこと（岐須美美命）」</ref> *<ref name="古代神祇-きしみみ">『日本古代神祇事典』p340「きしみみのみこと（研耳命）」</ref> Not in use-->
<!-- Not in use *<ref name="神仏の辞典-きすみみ">『日本の神仏の辞典』p400「きすみみのみこと【岐須美美命】」</ref> Not in use-->

}}

<!-- Note: The following pages were redirects]] before draftification: *[[Draft:Kisumimi]] *[[Kisumimi-no-Mikoto]] --> {{More citations needed|date=October 2021|talk=Untranslated refs in the reflist}} {{Infobox noble|reign-type=[[神武天皇]]の{{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|皇子|vi|Hoàng tử|zh|皇子|ko|황자|lt=Imperial Prince|12=draft}}|birth_date=unknown|death_place=[[Yamato Province]]|father=[[Jimmu]]|mother=[[Ahiratsu-hime]]}}'''Kisumimi-no-mikoto'''<ref>『日本古代神祇事典』p341「きすみみのみこと（岐須美美命）」</ref> is a character in [[Japanese mythology]], a male deity and the son of the first Emperor [[Emperor Jimmu|Jimmu]].<ref>『日本の神仏の辞典』p400「きすみみのみこと【岐須美美命】」</ref><ref>『日本の神様読み解き事典』p118「岐須美美命」</ref> He is the younger brother of [[Tagishimimi]] and some speculate they were the same person.<ref>{{Cite web |last=日本人名大辞典+Plus |first=デジタル版 |title=岐須美美命とは |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B2%90%E9%A0%88%E7%BE%8E%E7%BE%8E%E5%91%BD-1069200 |access-date=2022-01-12 |website=コトバンク |language=ja}}</ref>
== References in the Kiki == The [[Nihon Shoki]] simple describes him and Tagishimimi as being born to [[Ahiratsu-hime]] and [[Emperor Jimmu]].<ref>{{Citation |title=Book III |url=https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Nihongi:_Chronicles_of_Japan_from_the_Earliest_Times_to_A.D._697/Book_III |work=Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697, Volume 1 |access-date=2023-03-09}}</ref> without further mention
== Genealogy ==
{{A genealogical tree based on the Kojiki}}
== Footnotes == === Notes === {{notelist}}
=== Sources === {{Reflist|30em|refs=<!-- --> <!-- Not in use *<ref name="日本神名辞典-岐須">『日本神名辞典』p151「岐須美美命」</ref> Not in use-->
<!-- Not in use *<ref name="古代神祇-きすみみ">『日本古代神祇事典』p341「きすみみのみこと（岐須美美命）」</ref> *<ref name="古代神祇-きしみみ">『日本古代神祇事典』p340「きしみみのみこと（研耳命）」</ref> Not in use-->
<!-- Not in use *<ref name="神仏の辞典-きすみみ">『日本の神仏の辞典』p400「きすみみのみこと【岐須美美命】」</ref> Not in use-->
<!-- Not in use *<ref name="歴代天皇紀-神武">『図説 歴代天皇紀』p37-41「神武天皇」</ref> Not in use-->
<!-- Not in use *<ref name="歴代皇后-神武">『歴代皇后人物系譜総覧』,p26-27「初代 神武天皇 皇后 媛蹈韛五十鈴媛命」</ref> Not in use-->
<!-- Not in use *<ref name="読み解き事典-岐須美美命">『日本の神様読み解き事典』p118「岐須美美命」</ref> Not in use-->
<!-- Not in use *<ref name="学研2015">『古事記と日本の神々がわかる本』p90-91「イスケヨリヒメの物語」</ref> Not in use-->

}}
=== Bibliographic information ===
* "Dictionary of Japanese Divine Names", Jinja-Shimpo-Sha, 1994, 1995 (2nd edition), ISBN 4-915265-66-8 * "Dictionary of Japan's Ancient Shinto Gods", Kazunori Yoshida/editor, Chunichi Press, 2000, ISBN 4-88519-158-0 * "Dictionary of Japanese Gods and Buddha", Takehiko Oshima, Minoru Sonoda, Fumio Keimuro, Setsu Yamamoto (eds.), Taishukan Shoten, 2001, ISBN 4-469-01268-8 * "Nihon no Kami Yomiwake Jiten" (An Encyclopedia of Japanese Gods), Kenji Kawaguchi/editor, Kashiwa Shobo, 1999, 2009 (9th printing), ISBN 4-7601-1824-1 * "Kojiki to Nihon no Kami ga Kunderu Hon" (The Book of Ancient Matters and Understanding Japanese Gods), Kunihiro Yoshida, Gakken Publishing, 2015, ISBN 978-4-05-406340-2 * "Illustrated Chronicle of the Rekiyo Emperors", Edited by Masao Mitobe, Kazuo Higo, Shizuko Akagi, Shigetaka Fukuchi, [[Akita Shoten]], 1989, ISBN 4-253-00297-8 * "A Genealogical Directory of the Empresses of the Rekishi Era" (Bessatsu Rekishi Yomihon 24, Vol. 27, No. 29, 618), edited by Minoru Sato, Shinninjin Oraisha, 2002 * "The History of Hyuga Province: An Ancient History" by Sadakichi Kida, Toyo-do, 1943

<!-- Not in use *<ref name="古代神祇-きすみみ">『日本古代神祇事典』p341「きすみみのみこと（岐須美美命）」</ref> *<ref name="古代神祇-きしみみ">『日本古代神祇事典』p340「きしみみのみこと（研耳命）」</ref> Not in use-->
<!-- Not in use *<ref name="神仏の辞典-きすみみ">『日本の神仏の辞典』p400「きすみみのみこと【岐須美美命】」</ref> Not in use-->
<!-- Not in use *<ref name="歴代天皇紀-神武">『図説 歴代天皇紀』p37-41「神武天皇」</ref> Not in use-->

<!-- Not in use *<ref name="読み解き事典-岐須美美命">『日本の神様読み解き事典』p118「岐須美美命」</ref> Not in use-->

}}
=== Bibliographic information ===
* "Dictionary of Japanese Divine Names", Jinja-Shimpo-Sha, 1994, 1995 (2nd edition), ISBN 4-915265-66-8 * "Dictionary of Japan's Ancient Shinto Gods", Kazunori Yoshida/editor, Chunichi Press, 2000, ISBN 4-88519-158-0 * "Dictionary of Japanese Gods and Buddha", Takehiko Oshima, Minoru Sonoda, Fumio Keimuro, Setsu Yamamoto (eds.), Taishukan Shoten, 2001, ISBN 4-469-01268-8 * "Nihon no Kami Yomiwake Jiten" (An Encyclopedia of Japanese Gods), Kenji Kawaguchi/editor, Kashiwa Shobo, 1999, 2009 (9th printing), ISBN 4-7601-1824-1 * "Kojiki to Nihon no Kami ga Kunderu Hon" (The Book of Ancient Matters and Understanding Japanese Gods), Kunihiro Yoshida, Gakken Publishing, 2015, ISBN 978-4-05-406340-2 * "Illustrated Chronicle of the Rekiyo Emperors", Edited by Masao Mitobe, Kazuo Higo, Shizuko Akagi, Shigetaka Fukuchi, [[Akita Shoten]], 1989, ISBN 4-253-00297-8 * "A Genealogical Directory of the Empresses of the Rekishi Era" (Bessatsu Rekishi Yomihon 24, Vol. 27, No. 29, 618), edited by Minoru Sato, Shinninjin Oraisha, 2002 * "The History of Hyuga Province: An Ancient History" by Sadakichi Kida, Toyo-do, 1943
{{draft categories| [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects with bad categories]] [[Category:to [[Kisumimi]] [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kisumimi]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kisumimi]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] }} [[en:Kisumimi]] [[sn:Kisumimi]]

{{Short description|Character in Japanese mythology }}
<!-- Note: The following pages were redirects]] before draftification: *[[Draft:Kisumimi]] *[[Kisumimi-no-Mikoto]] --> {{More citations needed|date=October 2021|talk=Untranslated refs in the reflist}} {{Infobox noble|reign-type=[[神武天皇]]の{{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|皇子|vi|Hoàng tử|zh|皇子|ko|황자|lt=Imperial Prince|12=draft}}|birth_date=unknown|death_place=[[Yamato Province]]|father=[[Jimmu]]|mother=[[Ahiratsu-hime]]}}'''Kisumimi-no-mikoto'''<ref>『日本古代神祇事典』p341「きすみみのみこと（岐須美美命）」</ref> is a character in [[Japanese mythology]], a male deity and the son of the first Emperor [[Emperor Jimmu|Jimmu]].<ref>『日本の神仏の辞典』p400「きすみみのみこと【岐須美美命】」</ref><ref>『日本の神様読み解き事典』p118「岐須美美命」</ref> He is the younger brother of [[Tagishimimi]] and some speculate they were the same person.<ref>{{Cite web |last=日本人名大辞典+Plus |first=デジタル版 |title=岐須美美命とは |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B2%90%E9%A0%88%E7%BE%8E%E7%BE%8E%E5%91%BD-1069200 |access-date=2022-01-12 |website=コトバンク |language=ja}}</ref>
== References in the Kiki == The [[Nihon Shoki]] simple describes him and Tagishimimi as being born to [[Ahiratsu-hime]] and [[Emperor Jimmu]].<ref>{{Citation |title=Book III |url=https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Nihongi:_Chronicles_of_Japan_from_the_Earliest_Times_to_A.D._697/Book_III |work=Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697, Volume 1 |access-date=2023-03-09}}</ref> without further mention
== Genealogy ==
{{A genealogical tree based on the Kojiki}}
== Footnotes == === Notes === {{notelist}}
=== Sources === {{Reflist|30em|refs=<!-- --> <!-- Not in use *<ref name="日本神名辞典-岐須">『日本神名辞典』p151「岐須美美命」</ref> Not in use-->
<!-- Not in use *<ref name="古代神祇-きすみみ">『日本古代神祇事典』p341「きすみみのみこと（岐須美美命）」</ref> *<ref name="古代神祇-きしみみ">『日本古代神祇事典』p340「きしみみのみこと（研耳命）」</ref> Not in use-->
<!-- Not in use *<ref name="神仏の辞典-きすみみ">『日本の神仏の辞典』p400「きすみみのみこと【岐須美美命】」</ref> Not in use-->
<!-- Not in use *<ref name="歴代天皇紀-神武">『図説 歴代天皇紀』p37-41「神武天皇」</ref> Not in use-->
<!-- Not in use *<ref name="歴代皇后-神武">『歴代皇后人物系譜総覧』,p26-27「初代 神武天皇 皇后 媛蹈韛五十鈴媛命」</ref> Not in use-->
<!-- Not in use *<ref name="読み解き事典-岐須美美命">『日本の神様読み解き事典』p118「岐須美美命」</ref> Not in use-->
<!-- Not in use *<ref name="学研2015">『古事記と日本の神々がわかる本』p90-91「イスケヨリヒメの物語」</ref> Not in use-->
<!-- Not in use *<ref name="日本人名大辞典＋">『デジタル版 日本人名大辞典+Plus』,[[講談社]],2015,「媛蹈韛五十鈴媛命」[https://kotobank.jp/word/岐須美美命-1069200 コトバンク版] 2018th year 8月11日閲覧。</ref> Not in use-->
}}
=== Bibliographic information ===
* "Dictionary of Japanese Divine Names", Jinja-Shimpo-Sha, 1994, 1995 (2nd edition), ISBN 4-915265-66-8 * "Dictionary of Japan's Ancient Shinto Gods", Kazunori Yoshida/editor, Chunichi Press, 2000, ISBN 4-88519-158-0 * "Dictionary of Japanese Gods and Buddha", Takehiko Oshima, Minoru Sonoda, Fumio Keimuro, Setsu Yamamoto (eds.), Taishukan Shoten, 2001, ISBN 4-469-01268-8 * "Nihon no Kami Yomiwake Jiten" (An Encyclopedia of Japanese Gods), Kenji Kawaguchi/editor, Kashiwa Shobo, 1999, 2009 (9th printing), ISBN 4-7601-1824-1 * "Kojiki to Nihon no Kami ga Kunderu Hon" (The Book of Ancient Matters and Understanding Japanese Gods), Kunihiro Yoshida, Gakken Publishing, 2015, ISBN 978-4-05-406340-2 * "Illustrated Chronicle of the Rekiyo Emperors", Edited by Masao Mitobe, Kazuo Higo, Shizuko Akagi, Shigetaka Fukuchi, [[Akita Shoten]], 1989, ISBN 4-253-00297-8 * "A Genealogical Directory of the Empresses of the Rekishi Era" (Bessatsu Rekishi Yomihon 24, Vol. 27, No. 29, 618), edited by Minoru Sato, Shinninjin Oraisha, 2002 * "The History of Hyuga Province: An Ancient History" by Sadakichi Kida, Toyo-do, 1943

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kisumimi]][[ja:岐須美美命]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|father={{ill|Jimmu|WD=Q200188}}|mother={{ill|Ahiratsuhime|WD=Q10919837}}|relatives={{ill|Emperor Suizei|WD=Q313321}}; {{ill|Hikoyai-no-mikoto|WD=Q11086120}}; {{ill|Kamuyaimimi-no-mikoto|WD=Q11588756}}; {{ill|Tagishimimi|WD=Q11075750}}|native_name=研耳}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q13257020]] Wikidata name: Kishimi Mino Mikoto [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q13257020]] Wikidata name: Kishimi Mino Mikoto [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Character in Japanese mythology }}
{{More citations needed|date=October 2021|talk=Untranslated refs in the reflist}} {{Infobox noble|reign-type=[[神武天皇]]の{{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|皇子|vi|Hoàng tử|zh|皇子|ko|황자|lt=Imperial Prince|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Prince|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}|birth_date=unknown|death_place=[[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]|father=[[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]|mother=[[:en:Ahiratsu-hime|Ahiratsu-hime]]}}'''Kisumimi-no-mikoto'''<ref>『日本古代神祇事典』p341「きすみみのみこと（岐須美美命）」</ref> is a character in [[Japanese mythology]], a male deity and the son of the first Emperor [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Jimmu]].<ref>『日本の神仏の辞典』p400「きすみみのみこと【岐須美美命】」</ref><ref>『日本の神様読み解き事典』p118「岐須美美命」</ref> He is the younger brother of [[:en:Tagishimimi|Tagishimimi]] and some speculate they were the same person.<ref>{{Cite web |last=日本人名大辞典+Plus |first=デジタル版 |title=岐須美美命とは |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B2%90%E9%A0%88%E7%BE%8E%E7%BE%8E%E5%91%BD-1069200 |access-date=2022-01-12 |website=コトバンク |language=ja}}</ref>
== References in the Kiki == The [[Nihon Shoki]] simple describes him and Tagishimimi as being born to [[:en:Ahiratsu-hime|Ahiratsu-hime]] and [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]].<ref>{{Citation |title=Book III |url=https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Nihongi:_Chronicles_of_Japan_from_the_Earliest_Times_to_A.D._697/Book_III |work=Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697, Volume 1 |access-date=2023-03-09}}</ref> without further mention
== Genealogy ==
{{A genealogical tree based on the Kojiki}}
== Footnotes == === Notes === {{notelist}}
=== Sources === {{Reflist|30em|refs=
}}
=== Bibliographic information ===
* "Dictionary of Japanese Divine Names", Jinja-Shimpo-Sha, 1994, 1995 (2nd edition), ISBN 4-915265-66-8 * "Dictionary of Japan's Ancient Shinto Gods", Kazunori Yoshida/editor, Chunichi Press, 2000, ISBN 4-88519-158-0 * "Dictionary of Japanese Gods and Buddha", Takehiko Oshima, Minoru Sonoda, Fumio Keimuro, Setsu Yamamoto (eds.), Taishukan Shoten, 2001, ISBN 4-469-01268-8 * "Nihon no Kami Yomiwake Jiten" (An Encyclopedia of Japanese Gods), Kenji Kawaguchi/editor, Kashiwa Shobo, 1999, 2009 (9th printing), ISBN 4-7601-1824-1 * "Kojiki to Nihon no Kami ga Kunderu Hon" (The Book of Ancient Matters and Understanding Japanese Gods), Kunihiro Yoshida, Gakken Publishing, 2015, ISBN 978-4-05-406340-2 * "Illustrated Chronicle of the Rekiyo Emperors", Edited by Masao Mitobe, Kazuo Higo, Shizuko Akagi, Shigetaka Fukuchi, [[:en:Akita Shoten|Akita Shoten]], 1989, ISBN 4-253-00297-8 * "A Genealogical Directory of the Empresses of the Rekishi Era" (Bessatsu Rekishi Yomihon 24, Vol. 27, No. 29, 618), edited by Minoru Sato, Shinninjin Oraisha, 2002 * "The History of Hyūga Province: An Ancient History" by Sadakichi Kida, Toyo-do, 1943

}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kisumimi]][[ja:岐須美美命]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|father={{ill|Jimmu|WD=Q200188}}|mother={{ill|Ahiratsuhime|WD=Q10919837}}|relatives={{ill|Emperor Suizei|WD=Q313321}}; {{ill|Hikoyai-no-mikoto|WD=Q11086120}}; {{ill|Kamuyaimimi-no-mikoto|WD=Q11588756}}; {{ill|Tagishimimi|WD=Q11075750}}|native_name=研耳}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q13257020]] Wikidata name: Kishimi Mino Mikoto [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q13257020]] Wikidata name: Kishimi Mino Mikoto [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Character in Japanese mythology }}
{{More citations needed|date=October 2021|talk=Untranslated refs in the reflist}} {{Infobox noble|reign-type=[[神武天皇]]の{{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|皇子|vi|Hoàng tử|zh|皇子|ko|황자|lt=Imperial Prince|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Prince|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}|birth_date=unknown|death_place=[[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]|father=[[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]|mother=[[:en:Ahiratsu-hime|Ahiratsu-hime]]}}'''Kisumimi-no-mikoto'''<ref>『日本古代神祇事典』p341「きすみみのみこと（岐須美美命）」</ref> is a character in [[Japanese mythology]], a male deity and the son of the first Emperor [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Jimmu]].<ref>『日本の神仏の辞典』p400「きすみみのみこと【岐須美美命】」</ref><ref>『日本の神様読み解き事典』p118「岐須美美命」</ref> He is the younger brother of [[:en:Tagishimimi|Tagishimimi]] and some speculate they were the same person.<ref>{{Cite web |last=日本人名大辞典+Plus |first=デジタル版 |title=岐須美美命とは |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B2%90%E9%A0%88%E7%BE%8E%E7%BE%8E%E5%91%BD-1069200 |access-date=2022-01-12 |website=コトバンク |language=ja}}</ref>
== References in the Kiki == The [[Nihon Shoki]] simple describes him and Tagishimimi as being born to [[:en:Ahiratsu-hime|Ahiratsu-hime]] and [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]].<ref>{{Citation |title=Book III |url=https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Nihongi:_Chronicles_of_Japan_from_the_Earliest_Times_to_A.D._697/Book_III |work=Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697, Volume 1 |access-date=2023-03-09}}</ref> without further mention
== Genealogy ==
{{A genealogical tree based on the Kojiki}}

}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kisumimi]][[ja:岐須美美命]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|father={{ill|Jimmu|WD=Q200188}}|mother={{ill|Ahiratsuhime|WD=Q10919837}}|relatives={{ill|Emperor Suizei|WD=Q313321}}; {{ill|Hikoyai-no-mikoto|WD=Q11086120}}; {{ill|Kamuyaimimi-no-mikoto|WD=Q11588756}}; {{ill|Tagishimimi|WD=Q11075750}}|native_name=研耳}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q13257020]] Wikidata name: Kishimi Mino Mikoto [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q13257020]] Wikidata name: Kishimi Mino Mikoto [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Character in Japanese mythology }}
{{More citations needed|date=October 2021|talk=Untranslated refs in the reflist}} {{Infobox noble|reign-type=[[神武天皇]]の{{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|皇子|vi|Hoàng tử|zh|皇子|ko|황자|lt=Imperial Prince|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Prince|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}|birth_date=unknown|death_place=[[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]|father=[[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]|mother=[[:en:Ahiratsu-hime|Ahiratsu-hime]]}}'''Kisumimi-no-mikoto'''<ref>『日本古代神祇事典』p341「きすみみのみこと（岐須美美命）」</ref> is a character in [[Japanese mythology]], a male deity and the son of the first Emperor [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Jimmu]].<ref>『日本の神仏の辞典』p400「きすみみのみこと【岐須美美命】」</ref><ref>『日本の神様読み解き事典』p118「岐須美美命」</ref> He is the younger brother of [[:en:Tagishimimi|Tagishimimi]] and some speculate they were the same person.<ref>{{Cite web |last=日本人名大辞典+Plus |first=デジタル版 |title=岐須美美命とは |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B2%90%E9%A0%88%E7%BE%8E%E7%BE%8E%E5%91%BD-1069200 |access-date=2022-01-12 |website=コトバンク |language=ja}}</ref>
== References in the Kiki == The [[Nihon Shoki]] simple describes him and Tagishimimi as being born to [[:en:Ahiratsu-hime|Ahiratsu-hime]] and [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]].<ref>{{Citation |title=Book III |url=https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Nihongi:_Chronicles_of_Japan_from_the_Earliest_Times_to_A.D._697/Book_III |work=Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697, Volume 1 |access-date=2023-03-09}}</ref> without further mention
== Genealogy ==
{{A genealogical tree based on the Kojiki}}
== Footnotes == === Notes === {{notelist}}
=== Sources === {{Reflist|30em|refs=
}}
=== Bibliographic information ===
* "Dictionary of Japanese Divine Names", Jinja-Shimpo-Sha, 1994, 1995 (2nd edition), ISBN 4-915265-66-8 * "Dictionary of Japan's Ancient Shinto Gods", Kazunori Yoshida/editor, Chunichi Press, 2000, ISBN 4-88519-158-0 * "Dictionary of Japanese Gods and Buddha", Takehiko Oshima, Minoru Sonoda, Fumio Keimuro, Setsu Yamamoto (eds.), Taishukan Shoten, 2001, ISBN 4-469-01268-8 * "Nihon no Kami Yomiwake Jiten" (An Encyclopedia of Japanese Gods), Kenji Kawaguchi/editor, Kashiwa Shobo, 1999, 2009 (9th printing), ISBN 4-7601-1824-1 * "Kojiki to Nihon no Kami ga Kunderu Hon" (The Book of Ancient Matters and Understanding Japanese Gods), Kunihiro Yoshida, Gakken Publishing, 2015, ISBN 978-4-05-406340-2 * "Illustrated Chronicle of the Rekiyo Emperors", Edited by Masao Mitobe, Kazuo Higo, Shizuko Akagi, Shigetaka Fukuchi, [[:en:Akita Shoten|Akita Shoten]], 1989, ISBN 4-253-00297-8 * "A Genealogical Directory of the Empresses of the Rekishi Era" (Bessatsu Rekishi Yomihon 24, Vol. 27, No. 29, 618), edited by Minoru Sato, Shinninjin Oraisha, 2002 * "The History of Hyūga Province: An Ancient History" by Sadakichi Kida, Toyo-do, 1943

}}

== References in the Kiki == The [[Nihon Shoki]] simple describes him and Tagishimimi as being born to [[:en:Ahiratsu-hime|Ahiratsu-hime]] and [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]].<ref>{{Citation |title=Book III |url=https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Nihongi:_Chronicles_of_Japan_from_the_Earliest_Times_to_A.D._697/Book_III |work=Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697, Volume 1 |access-date=2023-03-09}}</ref> without further mention

=== Bibliographic information ===
* "Dictionary of Japanese Divine Names", Jinja-Shimpo-Sha, 1994, 1995 (2nd edition), ISBN 4-915265-66-8 * "Dictionary of Japan's Ancient Shinto Gods", Kazunori Yoshida/editor, Chunichi Press, 2000, ISBN 4-88519-158-0 * "Dictionary of Japanese Gods and Buddha", Takehiko Oshima, Minoru Sonoda, Fumio Keimuro, Setsu Yamamoto (eds.), Taishukan Shoten, 2001, ISBN 4-469-01268-8 * "Nihon no Kami Yomiwake Jiten" (An Encyclopedia of Japanese Gods), Kenji Kawaguchi/editor, Kashiwa Shobo, 1999, 2009 (9th printing), ISBN 4-7601-1824-1 * "Kojiki to Nihon no Kami ga Kunderu Hon" (The Book of Ancient Matters and Understanding Japanese Gods), Kunihiro Yoshida, Gakken Publishing, 2015, ISBN 978-4-05-406340-2 * "Illustrated Chronicle of the Rekiyo Emperors", Edited by Masao Mitobe, Kazuo Higo, Shizuko Akagi, Shigetaka Fukuchi, [[:en:Akita Shoten|Akita Shoten]], 1989, ISBN 4-253-00297-8 * "A Genealogical Directory of the Empresses of the Rekishi Era" (Bessatsu Rekishi Yomihon 24, Vol. 27, No. 29, 618), edited by Minoru Sato, Shinninjin Oraisha, 2002 * "The History of Hyūga Province: An Ancient History" by Sadakichi Kida, Toyo-do, 1943

* "Dictionary of Japanese Divine Names", Jinja-Shimpo-Sha, 1994, 1995 (2nd edition), ISBN 4-915265-66-8 * "Dictionary of Japan's Ancient Shinto Gods", Kazunori Yoshida/editor, Chunichi Press, 2000, ISBN 4-88519-158-0 * "Dictionary of Japanese Gods and Buddha", Takehiko Oshima, Minoru Sonoda, Fumio Keimuro, Setsu Yamamoto (eds.), Taishukan Shoten, 2001, ISBN 4-469-01268-8 * "Nihon no Kami Yomiwake Jiten" (An Encyclopedia of Japanese Gods), Kenji Kawaguchi/editor, Kashiwa Shobo, 1999, 2009 (9th printing), ISBN 4-7601-1824-1 * "Kojiki to Nihon no Kami ga Kunderu Hon" (The Book of Ancient Matters and Understanding Japanese Gods), Kunihiro Yoshida, Gakken Publishing, 2015, ISBN 978-4-05-406340-2 * "Illustrated Chronicle of the Rekiyo Emperors", Edited by Masao Mitobe, Kazuo Higo, Shizuko Akagi, Shigetaka Fukuchi, [[:en:Akita Shoten|Akita Shoten]], 1989, ISBN 4-253-00297-8 * "A Genealogical Directory of the Empresses of the Rekishi Era" (Bessatsu Rekishi Yomihon 24, Vol. 27, No. 29, 618), edited by Minoru Sato, Shinninjin Oraisha, 2002 * "The History of Hyūga Province: An Ancient History" by Sadakichi Kida, Toyo-do, 1943

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kisumimi]][[ja:岐須美美命]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|father={{ill|Jimmu|WD=Q200188}}|mother={{ill|Ahiratsuhime|WD=Q10919837}}|relatives={{ill|Emperor Suizei|WD=Q313321}}; {{ill|Hikoyai-no-mikoto|WD=Q11086120}}; {{ill|Kamuyaimimi-no-mikoto|WD=Q11588756}}; {{ill|Tagishimimi|WD=Q11075750}}|native_name=研耳}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q13257020]] Wikidata name: Kishimi Mino Mikoto [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q13257020]] Wikidata name: Kishimi Mino Mikoto [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Character in Japanese mythology }}
{{More citations needed|date=October 2021|talk=Untranslated refs in the reflist}} {{Infobox noble|reign-type=[[神武天皇]]の{{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|皇子|vi|Hoàng tử|zh|皇子|ko|황자|lt=Imperial Prince|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Prince|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}|birth_date=unknown|death_place=[[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]|father=[[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]|mother=[[:en:Ahiratsu-hime|Ahiratsu-hime]]}}'''Kisumimi-no-mikoto'''<ref>『日本古代神祇事典』p341「きすみみのみこと（岐須美美命）」</ref> is a character in [[Japanese mythology]], a male deity and the son of the first Emperor [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Jimmu]].<ref>『日本の神仏の辞典』p400「きすみみのみこと【岐須美美命】」</ref><ref>『日本の神様読み解き事典』p118「岐須美美命」</ref> He is the younger brother of [[:en:Tagishimimi|Tagishimimi]] and some speculate they were the same person.<ref>{{Cite web |last=日本人名大辞典+Plus |first=デジタル版 |title=岐須美美命とは |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B2%90%E9%A0%88%E7%BE%8E%E7%BE%8E%E5%91%BD-1069200 |access-date=2022-01-12 |website=コトバンク |language=ja}}</ref>
== References in the Kiki == The [[Nihon Shoki]] simple describes him and Tagishimimi as being born to [[:en:Ahiratsu-hime|Ahiratsu-hime]] and [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]].<ref>{{Citation |title=Book III |url=https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Nihongi:_Chronicles_of_Japan_from_the_Earliest_Times_to_A.D._697/Book_III |work=Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697, Volume 1 |access-date=2023-03-09}}</ref> without further mention
== Genealogy ==
{{A genealogical tree based on the Kojiki}}
== Footnotes == === Notes === {{notelist}}
=== Sources === {{Reflist|30em|refs=
}}
=== Bibliographic information ===
* "Dictionary of Japanese Divine Names", Jinja-Shimpo-Sha, 1994, 1995 (2nd edition), ISBN 4-915265-66-8 * "Dictionary of Japan's Ancient Shinto Gods", Kazunori Yoshida/editor, Chunichi Press, 2000, ISBN 4-88519-158-0 * "Dictionary of Japanese Gods and Buddha", Takehiko Oshima, Minoru Sonoda, Fumio Keimuro, Setsu Yamamoto (eds.), Taishukan Shoten, 2001, ISBN 4-469-01268-8 * "Nihon no Kami Yomiwake Jiten" (An Encyclopedia of Japanese Gods), Kenji Kawaguchi/editor, Kashiwa Shobo, 1999, 2009 (9th printing), ISBN 4-7601-1824-1 * "Kojiki to Nihon no Kami ga Kunderu Hon" (The Book of Ancient Matters and Understanding Japanese Gods), Kunihiro Yoshida, Gakken Publishing, 2015, ISBN 978-4-05-406340-2 * "Illustrated Chronicle of the Rekiyo Emperors", Edited by Masao Mitobe, Kazuo Higo, Shizuko Akagi, Shigetaka Fukuchi, [[:en:Akita Shoten|Akita Shoten]], 1989, ISBN 4-253-00297-8 * "A Genealogical Directory of the Empresses of the Rekishi Era" (Bessatsu Rekishi Yomihon 24, Vol. 27, No. 29, 618), edited by Minoru Sato, Shinninjin Oraisha, 2002 * "The History of Hyūga Province: An Ancient History" by Sadakichi Kida, Toyo-do, 1943

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kisumimi]][[ja:岐須美美命]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kisumimi]][[ja:岐須美美命]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|father={{ill|Jimmu|WD=Q200188}}|mother={{ill|Ahiratsuhime|WD=Q10919837}}|relatives={{ill|Emperor Suizei|WD=Q313321}}; {{ill|Hikoyai-no-mikoto|WD=Q11086120}}; {{ill|Kamuyaimimi-no-mikoto|WD=Q11588756}}; {{ill|Tagishimimi|WD=Q11075750}}|native_name=研耳}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q13257020]] Wikidata name: Kishimi Mino Mikoto [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q13257020]] Wikidata name: Kishimi Mino Mikoto [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Character in Japanese mythology }}
{{More citations needed|date=October 2021|talk=Untranslated refs in the reflist}} {{Infobox noble|reign-type=[[神武天皇]]の{{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|皇子|vi|Hoàng tử|zh|皇子|ko|황자|lt=Imperial Prince|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Prince|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}|birth_date=unknown|death_place=[[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]|father=[[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]|mother=[[:en:Ahiratsu-hime|Ahiratsu-hime]]}}'''Kisumimi-no-mikoto'''<ref>『日本古代神祇事典』p341「きすみみのみこと（岐須美美命）」</ref> is a character in [[Japanese mythology]], a male deity and the son of the first Emperor [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Jimmu]].<ref>『日本の神仏の辞典』p400「きすみみのみこと【岐須美美命】」</ref><ref>『日本の神様読み解き事典』p118「岐須美美命」</ref> He is the younger brother of [[:en:Tagishimimi|Tagishimimi]] and some speculate they were the same person.<ref>{{Cite web |last=日本人名大辞典+Plus |first=デジタル版 |title=岐須美美命とは |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B2%90%E9%A0%88%E7%BE%8E%E7%BE%8E%E5%91%BD-1069200 |access-date=2022-01-12 |website=コトバンク |language=ja}}</ref>
== References in the Kiki == The [[Nihon Shoki]] simple describes him and Tagishimimi as being born to [[:en:Ahiratsu-hime|Ahiratsu-hime]] and [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]].<ref>{{Citation |title=Book III |url=https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Nihongi:_Chronicles_of_Japan_from_the_Earliest_Times_to_A.D._697/Book_III |work=Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697, Volume 1 |access-date=2023-03-09}}</ref> without further mention
== Genealogy ==
{{A genealogical tree based on the Kojiki}}
== Footnotes == === Notes === {{notelist}}
=== Sources === {{Reflist|30em|refs=
}}
=== Bibliographic information ===
* "Dictionary of Japanese Divine Names", Jinja-Shimpo-Sha, 1994, 1995 (2nd edition), ISBN 4-915265-66-8 * "Dictionary of Japan's Ancient Shinto Gods", Kazunori Yoshida/editor, Chunichi Press, 2000, ISBN 4-88519-158-0 * "Dictionary of Japanese Gods and Buddha", Takehiko Oshima, Minoru Sonoda, Fumio Keimuro, Setsu Yamamoto (eds.), Taishukan Shoten, 2001, ISBN 4-469-01268-8 * "Nihon no Kami Yomiwake Jiten" (An Encyclopedia of Japanese Gods), Kenji Kawaguchi/editor, Kashiwa Shobo, 1999, 2009 (9th printing), ISBN 4-7601-1824-1 * "Kojiki to Nihon no Kami ga Kunderu Hon" (The Book of Ancient Matters and Understanding Japanese Gods), Kunihiro Yoshida, Gakken Publishing, 2015, ISBN 978-4-05-406340-2 * "Illustrated Chronicle of the Rekiyo Emperors", Edited by Masao Mitobe, Kazuo Higo, Shizuko Akagi, Shigetaka Fukuchi, [[:en:Akita Shoten|Akita Shoten]], 1989, ISBN 4-253-00297-8 * "A Genealogical Directory of the Empresses of the Rekishi Era" (Bessatsu Rekishi Yomihon 24, Vol. 27, No. 29, 618), edited by Minoru Sato, Shinninjin Oraisha, 2002 * "The History of Hyūga Province: An Ancient History" by Sadakichi Kida, Toyo-do, 1943

{{More citations needed|date=October 2021|talk=Untranslated refs in the reflist}} {{Infobox noble|reign-type=[[神武天皇]]の{{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|皇子|vi|Hoàng tử|zh|皇子|ko|황자|lt=Imperial Prince|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Prince|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}|birth_date=unknown|death_place=[[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]|father=[[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]|mother=[[:en:Ahiratsu-hime|Ahiratsu-hime]]}}'''Kisumimi-no-mikoto'''<ref>『日本古代神祇事典』p341「きすみみのみこと（岐須美美命）」</ref> is a character in [[Japanese mythology]], a male deity and the son of the first Emperor [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Jimmu]].<ref>『日本の神仏の辞典』p400「きすみみのみこと【岐須美美命】」</ref><ref>『日本の神様読み解き事典』p118「岐須美美命」</ref> He is the younger brother of [[:en:Tagishimimi|Tagishimimi]] and some speculate they were the same person.<ref>{{Cite web |last=日本人名大辞典+Plus |first=デジタル版 |title=岐須美美命とは |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B2%90%E9%A0%88%E7%BE%8E%E7%BE%8E%E5%91%BD-1069200 |access-date=2022-01-12 |website=コトバンク |language=ja}}</ref>
== References in the Kiki == The [[Nihon Shoki]] simple describes him and Tagishimimi as being born to [[:en:Ahiratsu-hime|Ahiratsu-hime]] and [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]].<ref>{{Citation |title=Book III |url=https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Nihongi:_Chronicles_of_Japan_from_the_Earliest_Times_to_A.D._697/Book_III |work=Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697, Volume 1 |access-date=2023-03-09}}</ref> without further mention
== Genealogy ==
{{A genealogical tree based on the Kojiki}}
== Footnotes == === Notes === {{notelist}}
=== Sources === {{Reflist|30em|refs=
}}
=== Bibliographic information ===
* "Dictionary of Japanese Divine Names", Jinja-Shimpo-Sha, 1994, 1995 (2nd edition), ISBN 4-915265-66-8 * "Dictionary of Japan's Ancient Shinto Gods", Kazunori Yoshida/editor, Chunichi Press, 2000, ISBN 4-88519-158-0 * "Dictionary of Japanese Gods and Buddha", Takehiko Oshima, Minoru Sonoda, Fumio Keimuro, Setsu Yamamoto (eds.), Taishukan Shoten, 2001, ISBN 4-469-01268-8 * "Nihon no Kami Yomiwake Jiten" (An Encyclopedia of Japanese Gods), Kenji Kawaguchi/editor, Kashiwa Shobo, 1999, 2009 (9th printing), ISBN 4-7601-1824-1 * "Kojiki to Nihon no Kami ga Kunderu Hon" (The Book of Ancient Matters and Understanding Japanese Gods), Kunihiro Yoshida, Gakken Publishing, 2015, ISBN 978-4-05-406340-2 * "Illustrated Chronicle of the Rekiyo Emperors", Edited by Masao Mitobe, Kazuo Higo, Shizuko Akagi, Shigetaka Fukuchi, [[:en:Akita Shoten|Akita Shoten]], 1989, ISBN 4-253-00297-8 * "A Genealogical Directory of the Empresses of the Rekishi Era" (Bessatsu Rekishi Yomihon 24, Vol. 27, No. 29, 618), edited by Minoru Sato, Shinninjin Oraisha, 2002 * "The History of Hyūga Province: An Ancient History" by Sadakichi Kida, Toyo-do, 1943

{{More citations needed|date=October 2021|talk=Untranslated refs in the reflist}} {{Infobox noble|reign-type=[[神武天皇]]の{{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|皇子|vi|Hoàng tử|zh|皇子|ko|황자|lt=Imperial Prince|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Prince|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}|birth_date=unknown|death_place={{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province}}|father={{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu}}|mother={{ill|Ahiratsu-hime|en|Ahiratsu-hime|WD=Q10919837|lt=Ahiratsu-hime}}}}'''Kisumimi-no-mikoto'''<ref>『日本古代神祇事典』p341「きすみみのみこと（岐須美美命）」</ref> is a character in [[Japanese mythology]], a male deity and the son of the first Emperor {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Jimmu}}.<ref>『日本の神仏の辞典』p400「きすみみのみこと【岐須美美命】」</ref><ref>『日本の神様読み解き事典』p118「岐須美美命」</ref> He is the younger brother of {{ill|Tagishimimi|en|Tagishimimi|WD=Q11075750|lt=Tagishimimi}} and some speculate they were the same person.<ref>{{Cite web |last=日本人名大辞典+Plus |first=デジタル版 |title=岐須美美命とは |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B2%90%E9%A0%88%E7%BE%8E%E7%BE%8E%E5%91%BD-1069200 |access-date=2022-01-12 |website=コトバンク |language=ja}}</ref>
== References in the Kiki == The [[Nihon Shoki]] simple describes him and Tagishimimi as being born to {{ill|Ahiratsu-hime|en|Ahiratsu-hime|WD=Q10919837|lt=Ahiratsu-hime}} and {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu}}.<ref>{{Citation |title=Book III |url=https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Nihongi:_Chronicles_of_Japan_from_the_Earliest_Times_to_A.D._697/Book_III |work=Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697, Volume 1 |access-date=2023-03-09}}</ref> without further mention
== Genealogy ==
{{A genealogical tree based on the Kojiki}}
== Footnotes == === Notes === {{notelist}}
=== Sources === {{Reflist|30em|refs=
}}
=== Bibliographic information ===
* "Dictionary of Japanese Divine Names", Jinja-Shimpo-Sha, 1994, 1995 (2nd edition), ISBN 4-915265-66-8 * "Dictionary of Japan's Ancient Shinto Gods", Kazunori Yoshida/editor, Chunichi Press, 2000, ISBN 4-88519-158-0 * "Dictionary of Japanese Gods and Buddha", Takehiko Oshima, Minoru Sonoda, Fumio Keimuro, Setsu Yamamoto (eds.), Taishukan Shoten, 2001, ISBN 4-469-01268-8 * "Nihon no Kami Yomiwake Jiten" (An Encyclopedia of Japanese Gods), Kenji Kawaguchi/editor, Kashiwa Shobo, 1999, 2009 (9th printing), ISBN 4-7601-1824-1 * "Kojiki to Nihon no Kami ga Kunderu Hon" (The Book of Ancient Matters and Understanding Japanese Gods), Kunihiro Yoshida, Gakken Publishing, 2015, ISBN 978-4-05-406340-2 * "Illustrated Chronicle of the Rekiyo Emperors", Edited by Masao Mitobe, Kazuo Higo, Shizuko Akagi, Shigetaka Fukuchi, {{ill|Akita Shoten|en|Akita Shoten|WD=Q419352|lt=Akita Shoten}}, 1989, ISBN 4-253-00297-8 * "A Genealogical Directory of the Empresses of the Rekishi Era" (Bessatsu Rekishi Yomihon 24, Vol. 27, No. 29, 618), edited by Minoru Sato, Shinninjin Oraisha, 2002 * "The History of Hyūga Province: An Ancient History" by Sadakichi Kida, Toyo-do, 1943

{{More citations needed|date=October 2021|talk=Untranslated refs in the reflist}} {{Infobox noble|reign-type=[[神武天皇]]の{{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|皇子|vi|Hoàng tử|zh|皇子|ko|황자|lt=Imperial Prince|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Prince|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}|birth_date=unknown|death_place={{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province}}|father={{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu}}|mother={{ill|Ahiratsu-hime|en|Ahiratsu-hime|WD=Q10919837|lt=Ahiratsu-hime}}}}'''Kisumimi-no-mikoto'''<ref>『日本古代神祇事典』p341「きすみみのみこと（岐須美美命）」</ref> is a character in [[Japanese mythology]], a male deity and the son of the first Emperor {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Jimmu}}.<ref>『日本の神仏の辞典』p400「きすみみのみこと【岐須美美命】」</ref><ref>『日本の神様読み解き事典』p118「岐須美美命」</ref> He is the younger brother of {{ill|Tagishimimi|en|Tagishimimi|WD=Q11075750|lt=Tagishimimi}} and some speculate they were the same person.<ref>{{Cite web |last=日本人名大辞典+Plus |first=デジタル版 |title=岐須美美命とは |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B2%90%E9%A0%88%E7%BE%8E%E7%BE%8E%E5%91%BD-1069200 |access-date=2022-01-12 |website=コトバンク |language=ja}}</ref>
== References in the Kiki == The [[Nihon Shoki]] simple describes him and Tagishimimi as being born to {{ill|Ahiratsu-hime|en|Ahiratsu-hime|WD=Q10919837|lt=Ahiratsu-hime}} and {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu}}.<ref>{{Citation |title=Book III |url=https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Nihongi:_Chronicles_of_Japan_from_the_Earliest_Times_to_A.D._697/Book_III |work=Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697, Volume 1 |access-date=2023-03-09}}</ref> without further mention
== Genealogy ==
{{A genealogical tree based on the Kojiki}}
== Footnotes == === Notes === {{notelist}}
=== Sources === {{Reflist|30em|refs=
}}
=== Bibliographic information ===
* "Dictionary of Japanese Divine Names", Jinja-Shimpo-Sha, 1994, 1995 (2nd edition), ISBN 4-915265-66-8 * "Dictionary of Japan's Ancient Shinto Gods", Kazunori Yoshida/editor, Chunichi Press, 2000, ISBN 4-88519-158-0 * "Dictionary of Japanese Gods and Buddha", Takehiko Oshima, Minoru Sonoda, Fumio Keimuro, Setsu Yamamoto (eds.), Taishukan Shoten, 2001, ISBN 4-469-01268-8 * "Nihon no Kami Yomiwake Jiten" (An Encyclopedia of Japanese Gods), Kenji Kawaguchi/editor, Kashiwa Shobo, 1999, 2009 (9th printing), ISBN 4-7601-1824-1 * "Kojiki to Nihon no Kami ga Kunderu Hon" (The Book of Ancient Matters and Understanding Japanese Gods), Kunihiro Yoshida, Gakken Publishing, 2015, ISBN 978-4-05-406340-2 * "Illustrated Chronicle of the Rekiyo Emperors", Edited by Masao Mitobe, Kazuo Higo, Shizuko Akagi, Shigetaka Fukuchi, {{ill|Akita Shoten|en|Akita Shoten|WD=Q419352|lt=Akita Shoten}}, 1989, ISBN 4-253-00297-8 * "A Genealogical Directory of the Empresses of the Rekishi Era" (Bessatsu Rekishi Yomihon 24, Vol. 27, No. 29, 618), edited by Minoru Sato, Shinninjin Oraisha, 2002 * "The History of Hyūga Province: An Ancient History" by Sadakichi Kida, Toyo-do, 1943

{{More citations needed|date=October 2021|talk=Untranslated refs in the reflist}} {{Infobox noble|reign-type=[[神武天皇]]の{{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|皇子|vi|Hoàng tử|zh|皇子|ko|황자|lt=Imperial Prince|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Prince|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}|birth_date=unknown|death_place={{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province}}|father={{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu}}|mother={{ill|Ahiratsu-hime|en|Ahiratsu-hime|WD=Q10919837|lt=Ahiratsu-hime}}}}'''Kisumimi-no-mikoto'''<ref>『日本古代神祇事典』p341「きすみみのみこと（岐須美美命）」</ref> is a character in [[Japanese mythology]], a male deity and the son of the first Emperor {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Jimmu}}.<ref>『日本の神仏の辞典』p400「きすみみのみこと【岐須美美命】」</ref><ref>『日本の神様読み解き事典』p118「岐須美美命」</ref> He is the younger brother of {{ill|Tagishimimi|en|Tagishimimi|WD=Q11075750|lt=Tagishimimi}} and some speculate they were the same person.<ref>{{Cite web |last=日本人名大辞典+Plus |first=デジタル版 |title=岐須美美命とは |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B2%90%E9%A0%88%E7%BE%8E%E7%BE%8E%E5%91%BD-1069200 |access-date=2022-01-12 |website=コトバンク |language=ja}}</ref>
== References in the Kiki == The [[Nihon Shoki]] simple describes him and Tagishimimi as being born to {{ill|Ahiratsu-hime|en|Ahiratsu-hime|WD=Q10919837|lt=Ahiratsu-hime}} and {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu}}.<ref>{{Citation |title=Book III |url=https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Nihongi:_Chronicles_of_Japan_from_the_Earliest_Times_to_A.D._697/Book_III |work=Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697, Volume 1 |access-date=2023-03-09}}</ref> without further mention
== Genealogy ==
{{A genealogical tree based on the Kojiki}}
== Footnotes == === Notes === {{notelist}}
=== Sources === {{Reflist|30em|refs=
}}
=== Bibliographic information ===
* "Dictionary of Japanese Divine Names", Jinja-Shimpo-Sha, 1994, 1995 (2nd edition), ISBN 4-915265-66-8 * "Dictionary of Japan's Ancient Shinto Gods", Kazunori Yoshida/editor, Chunichi Press, 2000, ISBN 4-88519-158-0 * "Dictionary of Japanese Gods and Buddha", Takehiko Oshima, Minoru Sonoda, Fumio Keimuro, Setsu Yamamoto (eds.), Taishukan Shoten, 2001, ISBN 4-469-01268-8 * "Nihon no Kami Yomiwake Jiten" (An Encyclopedia of Japanese Gods), Kenji Kawaguchi/editor, Kashiwa Shobo, 1999, 2009 (9th printing), ISBN 4-7601-1824-1 * "Kojiki to Nihon no Kami ga Kunderu Hon" (The Book of Ancient Matters and Understanding Japanese Gods), Kunihiro Yoshida, Gakken Publishing, 2015, ISBN 978-4-05-406340-2 * "Illustrated Chronicle of the Rekiyo Emperors", Edited by Masao Mitobe, Kazuo Higo, Shizuko Akagi, Shigetaka Fukuchi, {{ill|Akita Shoten|en|Akita Shoten|WD=Q419352|lt=Akita Shoten}}, 1989, ISBN 4-253-00297-8 * "A Genealogical Directory of the Empresses of the Rekishi Era" (Bessatsu Rekishi Yomihon 24, Vol. 27, No. 29, 618), edited by Minoru Sato, Shinninjin Oraisha, 2002 * "The History of Hyūga Province: An Ancient History" by Sadakichi Kida, Toyo-do, 1943

* "Dictionary of Japanese Divine Names", Jinja-Shimpo-Sha, 1994, 1995 (2nd edition), ISBN 4-915265-66-8 * "Dictionary of Japan's Ancient Shinto Gods", Kazunori Yoshida/editor, Chunichi Press, 2000, ISBN 4-88519-158-0 * "Dictionary of Japanese Gods and Buddha", Takehiko Oshima, Minoru Sonoda, Fumio Keimuro, Setsu Yamamoto (eds.), Taishukan Shoten, 2001, ISBN 4-469-01268-8 * "Nihon no Kami Yomiwake Jiten" (An Encyclopedia of Japanese Gods), Kenji Kawaguchi/editor, Kashiwa Shobo, 1999, 2009 (9th printing), ISBN 4-7601-1824-1 * "Kojiki to Nihon no Kami ga Kunderu Hon" (The Book of Ancient Matters and Understanding Japanese Gods), Kunihiro Yoshida, Gakken Publishing, 2015, ISBN 978-4-05-406340-2 * "Illustrated Chronicle of the Rekiyo Emperors", Edited by Masao Mitobe, Kazuo Higo, Shizuko Akagi, Shigetaka Fukuchi, {{ill|Akita Shoten|en|Akita Shoten|WD=Q419352|lt=Akita Shoten}}, 1989, ISBN 4-253-00297-8 * "A Genealogical Directory of the Empresses of the Rekishi Era" (Bessatsu Rekishi Yomihon 24, Vol. 27, No. 29, 618), edited by Minoru Sato, Shinninjin Oraisha, 2002 * "The History of Hyūga Province: An Ancient History" by Sadakichi Kida, Toyo-do, 1943

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kisumimi]][[ja:岐須美美命]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kisumimi]][[ja:岐須美美命]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kisumimi]][[ja:岐須美美命]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17|1=Japan}}|father={{ill|Jimmu|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}}|mother={{ill|Ahiratsuhime|WD=Q10919837|1=Ahiratsu-hime}}|relatives={{ill|Emperor Suizei|WD=Q313321|1=Emperor Suizei}}; {{ill|Hikoyai-no-mikoto|WD=Q11086120|1=Hikoyai}}; {{ill|Kamuyaimimi-no-mikoto|WD=Q11588756|1=Kamuyaimimi}}; {{ill|Tagishimimi|WD=Q11075750|1=Tagishimimi}}|native_name=研耳}}
{{Short description|Character in Japanese mythology }}
{{More citations needed|date=October 2021|talk=Untranslated refs in the reflist}} {{Infobox noble|reign-type=[[神武天皇]]の{{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|皇子|vi|Hoàng tử|zh|皇子|ko|황자|lt=Imperial Prince|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Prince|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}|birth_date=unknown|death_place={{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province|1=Yamato Province}}|father={{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu|1=Emperor Jimmu}}|mother={{ill|Ahiratsu-hime|en|Ahiratsu-hime|WD=Q10919837|lt=Ahiratsu-hime|1=Ahiratsu-hime}}}}'''Kisumimi-no-mikoto'''<ref>『日本古代神祇事典』p341「きすみみのみこと（岐須美美命）」</ref> is a character in [[Japanese mythology]], a male deity and the son of the first Emperor {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Jimmu|1=Emperor Jimmu}}.<ref>『日本の神仏の辞典』p400「きすみみのみこと【岐須美美命】」</ref><ref>『日本の神様読み解き事典』p118「岐須美美命」</ref> He is the younger brother of {{ill|Tagishimimi|en|Tagishimimi|WD=Q11075750|lt=Tagishimimi|1=Tagishimimi}} and some speculate they were the same person.<ref>{{Cite web |last=日本人名大辞典+Plus |first=デジタル版 |title=岐須美美命とは |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B2%90%E9%A0%88%E7%BE%8E%E7%BE%8E%E5%91%BD-1069200 |access-date=2022-01-12 |website=コトバンク |language=ja}}</ref>
== References in the Kiki == The [[Nihon Shoki]] simple describes him and Tagishimimi as being born to {{ill|Ahiratsu-hime|en|Ahiratsu-hime|WD=Q10919837|lt=Ahiratsu-hime|1=Ahiratsu-hime}} and {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu|1=Emperor Jimmu}}.<ref>{{Citation |title=Book III |url=https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Nihongi:_Chronicles_of_Japan_from_the_Earliest_Times_to_A.D._697/Book_III |work=Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697, Volume 1 |access-date=2023-03-09}}</ref> without further mention
== Genealogy ==
{{A genealogical tree based on the Kojiki}}
== Footnotes == === Notes === {{notelist}}
=== Sources === {{Reflist|30em|refs=
}}
=== Bibliographic information ===
* "Dictionary of Japanese Divine Names", Jinja-Shimpo-Sha, 1994, 1995 (2nd edition), ISBN 4-915265-66-8 * "Dictionary of Japan's Ancient Shinto Gods", Kazunori Yoshida/editor, Chunichi Press, 2000, ISBN 4-88519-158-0 * "Dictionary of Japanese Gods and Buddha", Takehiko Oshima, Minoru Sonoda, Fumio Keimuro, Setsu Yamamoto (eds.), Taishukan Shoten, 2001, ISBN 4-469-01268-8 * "Nihon no Kami Yomiwake Jiten" (An Encyclopedia of Japanese Gods), Kenji Kawaguchi/editor, Kashiwa Shobo, 1999, 2009 (9th printing), ISBN 4-7601-1824-1 * "Kojiki to Nihon no Kami ga Kunderu Hon" (The Book of Ancient Matters and Understanding Japanese Gods), Kunihiro Yoshida, Gakken Publishing, 2015, ISBN 978-4-05-406340-2 * "Illustrated Chronicle of the Rekiyo Emperors", Edited by Masao Mitobe, Kazuo Higo, Shizuko Akagi, Shigetaka Fukuchi, {{ill|Akita Shoten|en|Akita Shoten|WD=Q419352|lt=Akita Shoten|1=Akita Shoten}}, 1989, ISBN 4-253-00297-8 * "A Genealogical Directory of the Empresses of the Rekishi Era" (Bessatsu Rekishi Yomihon 24, Vol. 27, No. 29, 618), edited by Minoru Sato, Shinninjin Oraisha, 2002 * "The History of Hyūga Province: An Ancient History" by Sadakichi Kida, Toyo-do, 1943

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kisumimi]][[ja:岐須美美命]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kisumimi]][[ja:岐須美美命]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kisumimi]][[ja:岐須美美命]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|qid=Q17|1=Japan}}|father={{ill|Jimmu|qid=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}}|mother={{ill|Ahiratsuhime|qid=Q10919837|1=Ahiratsu-hime}}|relatives={{ill|Emperor Suizei|qid=Q313321|1=Emperor Suizei}}; {{ill|Hikoyai-no-mikoto|qid=Q11086120|1=Hikoyai}}; {{ill|Kamuyaimimi-no-mikoto|qid=Q11588756|1=Kamuyaimimi}}; {{ill|Tagishimimi|qid=Q11075750|1=Tagishimimi}}|native_name=研耳}}
{{Short description|Character in Japanese mythology }}
{{More citations needed|date=October 2021|talk=Untranslated refs in the reflist}} {{Infobox noble|reign-type=[[神武天皇]]の{{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|皇子|vi|Hoàng tử|zh|皇子|ko|황자|lt=Imperial Prince|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Prince|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}|birth_date=unknown|death_place={{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|qid=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province|1=Yamato Province}}|father={{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|qid=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu|1=Emperor Jimmu}}|mother={{ill|Ahiratsu-hime|en|Ahiratsu-hime|qid=Q10919837|lt=Ahiratsu-hime|1=Ahiratsu-hime}}}}'''Kisumimi-no-mikoto'''<ref>『日本古代神祇事典』p341「きすみみのみこと（岐須美美命）」</ref> is a character in [[Japanese mythology]], a male deity and the son of the first Emperor {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|qid=Q200188|lt=Jimmu|1=Emperor Jimmu}}.<ref>『日本の神仏の辞典』p400「きすみみのみこと【岐須美美命】」</ref><ref>『日本の神様読み解き事典』p118「岐須美美命」</ref> He is the younger brother of {{ill|Tagishimimi|en|Tagishimimi|qid=Q11075750|lt=Tagishimimi|1=Tagishimimi}} and some speculate they were the same person.<ref>{{Cite web |last=日本人名大辞典+Plus |first=デジタル版 |title=岐須美美命とは |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B2%90%E9%A0%88%E7%BE%8E%E7%BE%8E%E5%91%BD-1069200 |access-date=2022-01-12 |website=コトバンク |language=ja}}</ref>
== References in the Kiki == The [[Nihon Shoki]] simple describes him and Tagishimimi as being born to {{ill|Ahiratsu-hime|en|Ahiratsu-hime|qid=Q10919837|lt=Ahiratsu-hime|1=Ahiratsu-hime}} and {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|qid=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu|1=Emperor Jimmu}}.<ref>{{Citation |title=Book III |url=https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Nihongi:_Chronicles_of_Japan_from_the_Earliest_Times_to_A.D._697/Book_III |work=Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697, Volume 1 |access-date=2023-03-09}}</ref> without further mention
